channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: TikTok refugees or propagandists? China’s RedNote users share mixed responses to U.S. influx
item.description: Users of the Chinese social media app RedNote welcomed “TikTok refugees” from the United States with selfies and messages on Wednesday, as Beijing said it encouraged stronger cultural ties with other countries in response to the sudden influx.
item.dc:creator: Reuters, Fast Company

item.title: Schumer pushes for TikTok ban delay as deadline looms
item.description: Senate Majority Leader Chuck Schumer (D-N.Y.) on Thursday called for a looming TikTok ban to be delayed to give the social media company more time to find an American buyer. "We aren’t against TikTok. We want TikTok to keep going.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: How Letterboxd went mainstream
item.description: In 2020, Letterboxd’s highest-rated film was Lee Isaac Chung’s quiet indie Minari, which made a mere $15.5 million globally. Four years later, Denis Villeneuve’s $714.4 million-making juggernaut Dune: Part Two tops the charts.
item.dc:creator: Henry Chandonnet, Fast Company

item.title: TikTok users are flocking to alternative apps like RedNote
item.description: TikTok is still three days away from a likely ban in the United States, but many users are already bidding the app farewell and seeking out alternatives.
item.dc:creator: CNN.com Wire Service, The Mercury News

item.title: Avery label maker confirms attack on its site, customer credit card info stolen
item.description: Avery was unknowingly hosting a credit card skimmer for half a year, exposing customer data to hackers.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: It’s time for Apple to press pause on its AI news summaries
item.description: Apple should shut off its AI summaries of news alerts until it can prevent them from spreading fake news. Anecdotes of iPhone users seeing weirdly phrased summaries of texts and other content are common. But getting news headlines wrong is another matter.
item.dc:creator: Mark Sullivan, Fast Company

item.title: Chief Data Officer: sotto i riflettori nella veste di strateghi aziendali
item.description: Con i dati ormai al centro di ogni aspetto del business, il Chief Data Officer [in inglese] è diventato un dirigente altamente strategico che, oggi, si concentra nell’aiutare l’azienda a sfruttare le informazioni come risorsa imprescindibile per ottenere risultati.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Digital Transformation in Prisons: How Kazakhstan is Leading the Way
item.description: Technology innovations have made the digital transformation of public services essential to enhancing safety, efficiency, and accountability.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Trump will 'find a way' to preserve TikTok, Waltz says
item.description: President-elect Trump will take action to preserve TikTok upon taking office, his incoming national security adviser said Wednesday, with a deadline looming for the app’s owner to sell or face a ban in the United States. Rep. Mike Waltz (R-Fla.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, The Hill

item.title: H-1B visa: South Bay executives must be re-charged with visa fraud, judges rule
item.description: Federal prosecutors claimed the two falsely stated in 85 visa applications that prospective H-1B holders would work on-site on internal projects at their San Jose computer chip business, PerfectVIPs.
item.dc:creator: Ethan Baron, The Mercury News

item.title: SpaceX launches U.S and Japanese landers to the moon
item.description: In a two-for-one moonshot, SpaceX launched a pair of lunar landers Wednesday for U.S. and Japanese companies looking to jump-start business on Earth’s dusty sidekick.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: Fox will stream next month's Super Bowl for free on Tubi
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Fox will stream the Super Bowl on Tubi for the first time, giving streamers and cord cutters a chance to watch the big game on Feb. 9 for free. Tubi was acquired by Fox Corporation in 2020. It is a free, ad-supported streaming service.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Microsoft 365: A guide to the updates
item.description: Microsoft 365 (and Office 365) subscribers get more frequent software updates than those who have purchased Office without a subscription, which means subscribers have access to the latest features, security patches, and bug fixes.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: SEC sues Elon Musk, saying he didn't disclose Twitter ownership on time before buying it
item.description: The U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission has sued billionaire Elon Musk, saying he failed to disclose his ownership of Twitter stock in a timely manner in early 2022, before buying the social media site.
item.dc:creator: BARBARA ORTUTAY, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: First 15 things to do or try first when you get a new iPhone
item.description: Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson: If you just bought a new iPhone, here’s what to do first when you fire it up.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: HPE beats Dell and Supermicro in $1B AI server deal with X
item.description: Hewlett Packard Enterprise (HPE) has signed a contract exceeding $1 billion to provide AI servers for X, the platform formerly known as Twitter, according to Bloomberg.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: New Brazil law restricts use of smartphones in elementary and high schools
item.description: SAO PAULO (AP) — Brazil's President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva on Monday signed a bill restricting the use of smartphones at school, following a global trend for such limitations. The move will impact students at elementary and high schools across the South American nation starting in February.
item.dc:creator: MAURICIO SAVARESE and DIARLEI RODRIGUES, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Red Hat Completes Acquisition of Neural Magic | Technology News
item.description: Red Hat completes acquisition of Neural Magic, a pioneer in software and algorithms that accelerate gen AI inference workloads.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Unauthorized drone flights double; 3 arrested during LA fires
item.description: The FAA instituted temporary flight restrictions in the area. Aircraft are used to drop water on the blazes.
item.dc:creator: Steph Whiteside, NewsNation

item.title: Britain's leader unveils AI strategy, including vow to rip up red tape hindering AI projects
item.description: LONDON (AP) — The British government pledged on Monday to rip up the red tape hindering construction of data centers that underpin artificial intelligence while promoting its “pro-innovation” approach to regulating the technology.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Yes, you should use AI coding assistants—but not like that
item.description: If you’re exhausted by the constantly changing AI landscape, you’re not alone. In a thoughtful post, Microsoft Research brainiac Victor Dibia captures the “particular kind of fatigue that comes from trying to match the unprecedented pace of AI advancement.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Hydrogen-powered rescue truck just smashed a world record, and it only spits out water
item.description: A truck powered by hydrogen traveled 1,806 miles on a single tank of hydrogen with zero emissions and set a new Guinness World Record.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: China’s newest humanoid robot is ready to serve like never before
item.description: A Chinese company has developed a new bipedal humanoid robot that stands over 5 feet tall and can tackle a wide range of tasks in different settings.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Prime Video's use of AI, Next Gen Stats on NFL games is helping viewers understand the game better
item.description: CULVER CITY, Calif. (AP) — The Los Angeles Rams defense appeared to be content to drop back in coverage and prevent San Francisco’s Brock Purdy from completing a Hail Mary on the final play of their Thursday night game on Dec. 12. The broadcast on Amazon Prime Video saw it differently, though.
item.dc:creator: JOE REEDY, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Oracle refuses to yield JavaScript trademark, Deno Land says
item.description: JavaScript runtime provider Deno Land’s efforts to get Oracle to yield the trademark for JavaScript have hit a snag, with Oracle refusing to voluntarily withdraw the trademark, Deno Land said.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Nvidia year in review
item.description: 2024 was Nvidia’s year. Its command of mindshare and market share was unequaled among tech vendors. Here’s a recap of some of the key events of 2024 that highlight just how powerful the world’s most dominant chip player is.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Supreme Court seems likely to uphold Jan. 19 TikTok shutdown
item.description: The case asks the high court to weigh the First Amendment against serious national security concerns.
item.dc:creator: Tom Dempsey, NewsNation

item.title: Why JavaScript’s still on top in 2025
item.description: Welcome to a new year of programming and the brand new monthly list of JavaScript stories just for developers! Among the highlights so far: Svelte and SvelteKit have seen a slew of incremental improvements, Astro.js 5.0 just hit, and the Next.js team is working on a composable caching mechanism.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: How to easily print photos from your phone
item.description: Most cameras allow you to instantly transfer images from a camera to a smartphone via Bluetooth, and you can print photos directly from your phone.
item.dc:creator: Fox News
